behaviors.description: Conversation history sent to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (history manager -> LLM request builder), not just pure slicing logic. Candidate Integration test: setup chatbot service with 15-message history and stub LLM transport that records payload; assert exactly last 10 messages were sent; catches real bug where service forgets to call trim before dispatch. Adjacent Unit candidate (trim function only) would miss wiring regressions. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; stub transport is sufficient, so System is disqualified by behavior text (only says 'passed to the LLM', not real HTTP behavior). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no dependence on model output quality and no multi-agentic chain, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test on the chat orchestration path: provide >10 history entries, invoke response generation with a fake LLM client, and assert the outgoing LLM payload contains only the newest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: Generated response addresses the customer's actual question and is not generic/off-topic.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model judgment/content quality; a stubbed response would always pass and hide the failure. Candidate Agentic test: setup real prompt+history set with gold relevance rubric; assert response semantically answers the asked question; catches real off-topic drift. Adjacent Integration candidate with canned model output only verifies plumbing and cannot detect relevance failures. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text is single-step ('generated response addresses...') and does not require sequential agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic evaluation test using real model calls over a fixed dataset of customer questions: score answer relevance/groundedness (human-labeled or judge rubric) and fail when responses are generic or off-topic beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects an overly formal response for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP: behavior requires a sequence of agentic decisions/actions (initial generation -> tone assessment -> regeneration trigger). Candidate Workflow test: run end-to-end with real model, seed prompts likely to produce formal draft for casual tier, assert tone-check flags it and second generation is invoked; catches failures in decision handoff and loop control. Adjacent Agentic single-step tone-classification test cannot verify that classification actually drives regeneration orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: Integration disqualified by behavior text 'identifies ... and triggers regeneration' because model-dependent identification governs next-step control flow.
behaviors.test_description: Workflow test that executes the full response pipeline with real LLM calls, captures first draft and tone-check decision, and asserts regeneration occurs when first draft is too formal for casual tier.

behaviors.description: Full exchange (user message, generated response, regenerations) is persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when orchestration, persistence layer, and DB schema interact (including multi-record regeneration chain). Candidate Integration test: execute chat flow with regeneration against test DB, assert all records exist with correct linkage/order/timestamp fields; catches transactional or mapping bugs. Adjacent Unit test of mapper/repository method alone misses cross-module write sequencing. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local DB wiring, so System not required; no AI output-quality assertion, so Agentic/Workflow not required for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test using a real test database (or local container/in-memory engine): run one conversation with regeneration and assert persisted rows include user input, rejected draft, accepted draft, and valid monotonic timestamps.

behaviors.description: If tone-triggered regeneration happens, only the second response is exposed to the customer; rejected draft is never shown.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is orchestration/state handoff, not model-quality. Candidate Integration test: stub first draft + tone flag + second draft through chat service/API boundary; assert customer-visible payload equals second draft and first appears only in internal logs; catches leakage bugs between regeneration state and response presenter. Adjacent Unit test of selector function could miss boundary leaks in controller/serializer. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic disqualified by behavior text ('second response replaces the first') because correctness is deterministic replacement logic, not whether model content is good.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test across generation orchestration and response presenter: force regeneration path and verify outbound customer response contains only regenerated text while original draft remains non-visible.
